{{Short description|SI unit of force}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Newton (unit)
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Force]], [[International System of Units]], [[Newton's laws of motion]]
| unit_system = [[SI derived unit]]
| unit_of = [[Force]]
| symbol = N
| named_after = [[Isaac Newton]]
| conversion = 1 N = 1 kg·m/s²
}}

The '''newton''' (symbol: '''N''') is the [[SI derived unit]] of [[force]]. It is named after [[Isaac Newton]] in recognition of his work on [[classical mechanics]], specifically [[Newton's second law|Newton's second law of motion]].

One newton is the force needed to accelerate one [[kilogram]] of [[mass]] at a rate of one [[metre per second squared]] in the direction of the applied force:

:<math>1\ \text{N} = 1\ \text{kg} \cdot \text{m} / \text{s}^2</math>

== Definition from Newton's second law ==
[[Newton's second law]] provides the definition:
:<math>F = ma</math>

So: <math>1\ \text{N} = 1\ \text{kg} \times 1\ \text{m/s}^2</math>

== Multiples and conversions ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Submultiples !! Value
|-
| micronewton (μN) || 10⁻⁶ N
|-
| millinewton (mN) || 10⁻³ N
|-
| centinewton (cN) || 10⁻² N
|}

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Multiples !! Value
|-
| kilonewton (kN) || 10³ N
|-
| meganewton (MN) || 10⁶ N
|-
| giganewton (GN) || 10⁹ N
|}

=== Common conversion factors ===
* 1 [[dyne]] = 10⁻⁵ N (CGS unit)
* 1 [[kilogram-force]] (kgf) = 9.80665 N
* 1 [[pound-force]] (lbf) ≈ 4.448 N
* 1 [[poundal]] ≈ 0.138 N

== Practical examples ==
* Weight of a 100 g apple on Earth: ~1 N
* Average bite force of a human: ~500 N
* Thrust of a [[Space Shuttle]] main engine at liftoff: ~1.8 MN
* Gravitational force between Earth and Moon: ~2 × 10²⁰ N

== Relation to other SI units ==
* <math>1\ \text{N} = 1\ \text{Pa} \cdot \text{m}^2</math> (pressure × area)
* <math>1\ \text{N} = 1\ \text{J/m}</math> (energy per distance)
* <math>1\ \text{N} = 1\ \text{W} \cdot \text{s/m}</math> (power × time / distance)

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Force]]
* [[International System of Units]]
* [[Isaac Newton]]
* [[Kilogram]]
* [[Newton's laws of motion]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Units of force]]
[[Category:SI derived units]]
[[Category:Isaac Newton]]
